description: Makes an HTTP GET request to a configurable base URL's delay endpoint with a specified delay and optional request ID header, and returns timing and response data. Reusable for any API that follows the httpbin delay pattern.

evaluation.validation_summary: Ctx2Skill-lite preview verifier contract. External corpus parse/audit keeps this ephemeral until a human approves import.

implementation.prompt_template: Make an HTTP GET request to {base_url}/delay/{delay} with optional header X-Request-ID: {request_id}. Measure the time from request start to response end. Include both the response body (as JSON) and the measured time in the output.

base_url.description: Base URL of the API (e.g., https://httpbin.org)

delay.description: Delay in seconds for the response

response_time.description: Measured time from request start to response end in seconds

postconditions: A response is received after approximately the requested delay.
The response status code is 200 OK.
If request_id was provided, it appears in the response headers.
preconditions: Network access to the base URL is available.
The delay parameter is a non-negative integer.
The base URL is a valid and reachable endpoint supporting the delay pattern.

name: make_delayed_request_api_doc_httpbin_delay

challenges.expected_evidence: Response received after approximately 5 seconds with status 200.
challenges.failure_signal: Response received too quickly (<4.5s) or too slowly (>5.5s), or status not 200.
rubric: The agent should make a GET request to the delay endpoint with delay=5.
The agent should measure the time taken from request start to response receipt.
The measured time should be between 4.5 and 5.5 seconds.
The response status code should be 200.
challenges.task: Call the delay endpoint with a delay of 5 seconds and verify the response time is within 10% of 5 seconds.

challenges.expected_evidence: Response JSON includes the exact header value under headers.X-Request-ID.
challenges.failure_signal: Header is missing, value differs, or response is not JSON.
rubric: The agent should include an X-Request-ID header with value 'test-123' in the request.
The agent should parse the response JSON.
The response should contain a 'headers' object with 'X-Request-ID' equal to 'test-123'.
challenges.task: Call the delay endpoint with a request_id header and verify the header value is echoed back in the response.

constraints: The delay parameter must be a non-negative integer or float (likely integer in path).
The base URL is for demonstration only and must be replaced with a real endpoint in production.
The response time may not be exactly the delay due to network latency.
examples: GET https://httpbin.org/delay/3 with header X-Request-ID: my-test returns after ~3 seconds.
facts: The endpoint is GET /delay/{delay} where {delay} is a path parameter representing the delay in seconds.
The API is hosted at https://example.invalid (placeholder URL).
The endpoint can accept an optional X-Request-ID header for request identification.
The response is a JSON object that typically echoes request details including headers.
procedures: To call: issue a GET request to {base_url}/delay/{delay} with an optional X-Request-ID header.
Measure response time to verify the delay is approximately as requested.
The response body contains keys like 'url', 'headers', 'origin', etc.
context_pack.summary: Focused httpbin API fixture providing a delayed response endpoint for timeout behavior testing. The GET /delay/{delay} endpoint returns a response after a specified delay (in seconds), and accepts an optional X-Request-ID header for request tracking.

ctx2skill_evidence.demo_scope: P0/P1 demo-paper implementation; not full long-horizon self-play training.

judge_results.rationale: Ctx2Skill-lite replay score meets the demo threshold.

selected_candidate.description: Makes an HTTP GET request to a configurable base URL's delay endpoint with a specified delay and optional request ID header, and returns timing and response data. Reusable for any API that follows the httpbin delay pattern.

base_url.description: Base URL of the API (e.g., https://httpbin.org)

delay.description: Delay in seconds for the response

selected_candidate.name: make_delayed_request

response_time.description: Measured time from request start to response end in seconds

postconditions: A response is received after approximately the requested delay.
The response status code is 200 OK.
If request_id was provided, it appears in the response headers.
preconditions: Network access to the base URL is available.
The delay parameter is a non-negative integer.

selected_candidate.prompt_template: Make an HTTP GET request to {base_url}/delay/{delay} with optional header X-Request-ID: {request_id}. Measure the time from request start to response end. Include both the response body (as JSON) and the measured time in the output.

ctx2skill_evidence.selected_reason: The 'make_delayed_request' skill is selected because it is more general and reusable: it accepts a configurable base_url, making it applicable to any API that follows the httpbin delay pattern, not just the placeholder example.invalid. Its input and output schemas are complete, preconditions and postconditions are clearly defined, and it provides timing information alongside the response body, which is necessary for both challenges. The higher score (0.85 vs 0.70) reflects its broader applicability and better alignment with the challenges.

creation_context.license_note: public API documentation normalized into a focused OpenAPI-style fixture; source URL recorded
creation_context.raw_content_preview:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Focused httpbin API fixture - httpbin-delay
  version: '1.0'
  description: Focused public API documentation excerpt normalized for SkillOS API-doc-to-skill testing.
servers:
  - url: https://example.invalid
paths:
  /delay/{delay}:
    get:
      operationId: httpbin_delay
      summary: Return a delayed response for timeout behavior testing.
      parameters:
        - name: request_id
          in: header
          required: false
          schema: {type: string}
